items.front: Helicopter/powered-lift departure after a preceding departure
items.back: The preceding departing helicopter or powered-lift aircraft must have left the takeoff area.

items.front: Helicopter/powered-lift departure after a preceding arrival
items.back: The preceding arriving helicopter or powered-lift aircraft must have taxied off the landing area.

items.front: Helicopter/powered-lift arrival after a preceding arrival
items.back: Do not land until the preceding arriving helicopter/powered-lift aircraft has come to a stop or taxied off the landing area.

items.front: Helicopter/powered-lift arrival after a preceding departure
items.back: Do not land until the preceding departing helicopter/powered-lift aircraft has left the landing area.

items.front: Sea-lane departure: only Category I aircraft involved
items.back: 1,500 feet minimum distance if distances can be determined by suitable landmarks.

items.front: Sea-lane departure: Category I aircraft preceded by Category II
items.back: 3,000 feet minimum distance if distances can be determined by suitable landmarks.

items.front: Sea-lane departure: either aircraft is Category III
items.back: 6,000 feet minimum distance if distances can be determined by suitable landmarks.

items.front: RNAV approach with RF leg: direct-to limitation
items.back: Do not clear aircraft direct to any waypoint beginning or within an RF leg.

items.front: TAA approach clearance: aircraft inside TAA boundary and at/above TAA minimum altitude
items.back: The aircraft is established on the approach and may be issued an approach clearance without an altitude restriction.

items.front: RNAV direct to IF: what must the SIAP identify?
items.back: The procedure must identify the intermediate fix with the letters IF, and radar monitoring must be provided to the IF.

items.front: DME arcs about same NAVAID: arcs 35 miles or less
items.back: 10 miles between different arcs, regardless of direction of flight.

items.front: DME arcs about same NAVAID: arcs more than 35 miles
items.back: 20 miles between different arcs, regardless of direction of flight.

items.front: DME arc to other protected airspace: arc 35 miles or less
items.back: 5 miles between the arc and the other protected airspace.

items.front: DME arc to other protected airspace: arc more than 35 miles
items.back: 10 miles between the arc and the other protected airspace.

items.front: Nonairway/nonroute direct courses: course change 15 degrees or less via NAVAIDs/radials
items.back: Protect 4 miles each side to 51 miles from the NAVAID, then expand at 4.5 degrees to 10 miles each side at 130 miles.

items.front: Overflown-side minima: 16-90 degree course change, FL 180 to FL 230 inclusive
items.back: 14 miles on the overflown side beginning at the point where the course changes.

items.front: Overflown-side minima: 16-90 degree course change, above FL 230 to FL 600 inclusive
items.back: 17 miles on the overflown side beginning at the point where the course changes.

items.front: Overflown-side minima: 91-180 degree course change, above FL 230 to FL 600 inclusive
items.back: 34 miles on the overflown side beginning at the point where the course changes.

items.front: RNAV diverging/crossing courses: route width 8 miles or less
items.back: Lateral separation exists when the RNAV aircraft is at least 4 miles beyond the point where protected airspace ceases to overlap.

items.front: RNAV diverging/crossing courses: route segment greater than 8 miles wide
items.back: Use 9 miles beyond the point where protected airspace ceases to overlap, unless the extended-distance case requires 15 miles.

items.front: Oceanic longitudinal separation: same track
items.back: Ensure estimated spacing between aircraft is not less than the applicable minimum required.

items.front: Oceanic longitudinal separation: crossing tracks
items.back: Ensure estimated spacing at the point of intersection is not less than the applicable minimum required.

items.front: Oceanic longitudinal separation: reciprocal tracks
items.back: Maintain vertical separation for a time interval equal to the applicable minimum before and after the aircraft are estimated to pass, unless discontinuation conditions are met.

items.front: Mach table: following aircraft faster by Mach 0.04, distance to fly 1201-1800 NM
items.back: 22 minutes required at the entry point.

items.front: Mach table: following aircraft faster by Mach 0.10, distance to fly 2401-3000 NM
items.back: 60 minutes required at the entry point.

items.front: Mach table: following aircraft faster by Mach 0.02, distance to fly 601-1200 NM
items.back: 14 minutes required at the entry point.

items.front: Offshore/oceanic same-direction transition climb-through: time minimum
items.back: 5 minutes minimum separation when all listed same-direction conditions are met.

items.front: Same-direction transition climb-through: altitude difference at clearance issue
items.back: The following aircraft must be separated by not more than 4,000 feet from the preceding aircraft when the climb clearance is issued.

items.front: Same-direction transition climb-through: timing after reporting point
items.back: The following aircraft must commence climb within 10 minutes after passing the exact reporting point or radar-observed position used by the preceding aircraft.

items.front: North American ADS-C criteria: 50 NM with RNP 10, RCP 240, RSP 180
items.back: Maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval is 27 minutes.

items.front: North American ADS-C criteria: 20 NM with RNP 4, RCP 240, RSP 180
items.back: Maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval is 192 seconds.

items.front: ADS-C same-track climb/descent: preceding aircraft same speed or faster
items.back: Minimum longitudinal distance is 15 NM.

items.front: ADS-C same-track climb/descent: following aircraft not more than Mach 0.02 faster
items.back: Minimum longitudinal distance is 25 NM.

items.front: ADS-C overdue report timing
items.back: If a periodic or waypoint-change event report is overdue by 3 minutes, obtain an ADS-C report; if no report is received within 6 minutes of when originally due, apply another form of separation.
